topic: Tenses Part 2 — Past Tense (Simple, Continuous, Perfect, Perfect Continuous)
keyPointsSummary: Simple Past: completed action in the past (I ate rice)
Past Continuous: ongoing action at a past moment (I was eating rice)
Past Perfect: action completed before another past action (I had eaten rice before he came)
Past Perfect Continuous: ongoing action before a past moment (I had been eating rice for an hour)

quickQuiz.hindi: जब वह आया, मैं खाना खा रहा था।
quickQuiz.answer: I was eating food when he came.

quickQuiz.hindi: उसके आने से पहले मैं खाना खा चुका था।
quickQuiz.answer: I had eaten food before he came.

quickQuiz.hindi: मैं एक घंटे से पढ़ रहा था।
quickQuiz.answer: I had been studying for an hour.

quickQuiz.hindi: वह पिछले साल स्कूल गई थी।
quickQuiz.answer: She went to school last year.

quickQuiz.hindi: जब मैं आया, वह काम कर रही थी।
quickQuiz.answer: She was working when I arrived.

quickQuiz.hindi: मेरे पहुँचने से पहले उसने काम खत्म कर लिया था।
quickQuiz.answer: She had finished her work before I arrived.

quickQuiz.hindi: वह दो घंटे से काम कर रही थी।
quickQuiz.answer: She had been working for two hours.

quickQuiz.hindi: जब मैंने फोन किया, वे खेल रहे थे।
quickQuiz.answer: They were playing when I called.

mustRememberRules: Simple Past: completed action in the past (I ate rice)
Past Continuous: ongoing action at a past moment (I was eating rice)
Past Perfect: action completed before another past action (I had eaten rice before he came)
Past Perfect Continuous: ongoing action before a past moment (I had been eating rice for an hour)
